info.title: Vexa API Gateway
info.description:
**Main entry point for the Vexa platform APIs.**

    Provides access to:
    - Bot Management (Starting/Stopping transcription bots)
    - Transcription Retrieval
    - User & Token Administration (Admin only)

    ## Authentication

    Two types of API keys are used:

    1.  **`X-API-Key`**: Required for all regular client operations (e.g., managing bots, getting transcripts). Obtain your key from an administrator.
    2.  **`X-Admin-API-Key`**: Required *only* for administrative endpoints (prefixed with `/admin`). This key is configured server-side.

    Include the appropriate header in your requests.

get.description: Provides a welcome message for the Vexa API Gateway.

get.summary: List recent meetings/bots for the user
get.description: Forward to meeting-api GET /bots — returns all meetings (active + completed).

post.summary: Request a new bot to join a meeting
post.description: Creates a new meeting record and launches a bot instance based on platform and native meeting ID.

requestBody.description: Specify the meeting platform, native ID, and optional bot name.

max_bot_time.title: Max Bot Time
max_bot_time.description: Absolute max bot lifetime in ms (server-enforced via scheduler)

max_wait_for_admission.title: Max Wait For Admission
max_wait_for_admission.description: Max time to wait for admission in ms

max_time_left_alone.title: Max Time Left Alone
max_time_left_alone.description: Max time left alone before leaving in ms

no_one_joined_timeout.title: No One Joined Timeout
no_one_joined_timeout.description: No one joined timeout in ms

AutomaticLeave.title: AutomaticLeave
AutomaticLeave.description:
Optional overrides for automatic-leave timeouts (milliseconds).

New field names: max_wait_for_admission, max_time_left_alone, max_bot_time.
Old field names (waiting_room_timeout, everyone_left_timeout) still accepted
for backward compatibility via model_validator.

Platform.title: Platform
Platform.description:
Platform identifiers for meeting platforms.
The value is the external API name, while the bot_name is what's used internally by the bot.

platform.description: Meeting platform. Required unless agent_enabled=true with no meeting.

native_meeting_id.title: Native Meeting Id
native_meeting_id.description: The platform-specific ID for the meeting (e.g., Google Meet code, Teams ID). Required unless agent_enabled=true with no meeting.

bot_name.title: Bot Name
bot_name.description: Optional name for the bot in the meeting

language.title: Language
language.description: Optional language code for transcription (e.g., 'en', 'es'). Forces this single language.

task.title: Task
task.description: Optional task for the transcription model (e.g., 'transcribe', 'translate')

recording_enabled.title: Recording Enabled
recording_enabled.description: Optional per-meeting override for recording persistence (true/false).

transcribe_enabled.title: Transcribe Enabled
transcribe_enabled.description: Optional per-meeting override for transcription processing (true/false).

passcode.title: Passcode
passcode.description: Optional passcode for the meeting (Teams short links; Zoom passcode screen; Jitsi room password)

meeting_url.title: Meeting Url
meeting_url.description: Meeting URL. When provided without native_meeting_id, the URL is parsed to extract platform, native_meeting_id, and passcode automatically. Supports Google Meet, Teams (all formats), and Zoom URLs.

teams_base_host.title: Teams Base Host
teams_base_host.description: Internal: Teams hostname for short enterprise URLs (e.g. 'teams.microsoft.com', 'gov.teams.microsoft.us'). Populated automatically by the MCP parser.

zoom_obf_token.title: Zoom Obf Token
zoom_obf_token.description: Optional one-time Zoom OBF token. If omitted for Zoom meetings, the backend will mint one from the user's stored Zoom OAuth connection.

voice_agent_enabled.title: Voice Agent Enabled
voice_agent_enabled.description: Enable voice agent (TTS, chat, screen share, avatar streaming) capabilities for this meeting

default_avatar_url.title: Default Avatar Url
default_avatar_url.description: Custom default avatar image URL for the bot's camera feed. Shown when no screen content is active. If omitted, the default Vexa logo is used.

automatic_leave.description: Optional overrides for automatic-leave timeouts (ms). Unset fields keep defaults.

agent_enabled.title: Agent Enabled
agent_enabled.description: Enable Claude agent in the bot container. Agent can control the browser, debug selectors, and modify code interactively.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: Bot mode: 'browser_session' for remote browser access, or None for default meeting mode.

video.title: Video
video.description: Enable video recording (Xvfb screen capture of the bot's browser view). Default: off. When true, sets recording_enabled=true and capture_modes=['audio', 'video']. Opt-in — leaving this false (or unset) gives audio-only recording, which is the expected default for transcription-focused deployments.

authenticated.title: Authenticated
authenticated.description: Use stored browser userdata for authenticated join. Requires prior browser_session setup.

workspaceGitRepo.title: Workspacegitrepo
workspaceGitRepo.description: Git repo URL for workspace setup in browser_session mode

workspaceGitToken.title: Workspacegittoken
workspaceGitToken.description: Git token for workspace repo access

dry_run.title: Dry Run
dry_run.description: Synthetic-test mode: create meeting without launching a real bot. Test driver drives lifecycle via internal callback endpoints. Gated by VEXA_ENV != 'production'.

delete.summary: Stop a bot for a specific meeting
delete.description: Stops the bot container associated with the specified platform and native meeting ID. Requires ownership via API key.

put.summary: Update configuration for an active bot
put.description: Updates the language and/or task for an active bot. Sends command via Bot Manager.

get.summary: Get status of running bots for the user
get.description: Retrieves a list of currently running bot containers associated with the authenticated user.

get.summary: Get a single meeting/bot by database ID
get.description: Forward to meeting-api GET /bots/{meeting_id}.

post.summary: Make the bot speak in a meeting
post.description: Sends text for TTS or raw audio to be played into the meeting via the bot's microphone.

schema.description: Request to make the bot speak in the meeting.

format.description: Audio format: wav, mp3, pcm, opus
format.title: Format

provider.description: TTS provider: piper (default, local), openai, cartesia, elevenlabs
provider.title: Provider

voice.description: Voice ID for TTS; auto chooses a local Piper voice from text language
voice.title: Voice

schema.title: SpeakRequest

requestBody.description: Text to speak (TTS) or audio URL/base64 to play.

delete.description: Stops any currently playing TTS or audio in the meeting.

post.summary: Send a chat message in the meeting
post.description: Sends a text message into the meeting chat via the bot.

schema.description: Request to send a message to the meeting chat.

text.description: Message text to send in the meeting chat
text.title: Text

schema.title: ChatSendRequest

get.summary: Read chat messages from the meeting
get.description: Returns chat messages captured by the bot from the meeting chat.

post.description: Displays an image, video, or URL via the bot's screen share in the meeting.

schema.description: Request to show content on screen (via screen share).

type.description: Content type: image, video, url, html
type.title: Type

url.description: URL of the content to display
url.title: Url

schema.title: ScreenContentRequest

requestBody.description: Content to display (image, video, or URL).

delete.description: Stops the bot's screen share and clears the displayed content.

put.description: Sets a custom avatar for the bot's camera feed. Shown when no screen content is active. Provide 'url' (image URL) or 'image_base64' (data URI). Use DELETE to revert to default.

delete.description: Resets the bot's avatar to the default Vexa logo.

post.summary: Trigger initial calendar sync after OAuth

get.summary: List recordings for the authenticated user
get.description: Returns a paginated list of recordings. Optionally filter by meeting_id.
Returns a single recording with its media files.

delete.description: Deletes a recording, its media files from storage, and all database rows.

get.description: Resolves the finalized master recording media for browser playback.

get.summary: Get download URL for a media file
get.description: Generates a presigned URL to download the specified media file.

get.summary: Download media bytes via API (local backend)
get.description: Streams media bytes through API. Primarily for local filesystem storage backend.
Returns the user's recording configuration.

put.description: Update the user's recording configuration (enable/disable, capture modes).

post.description: Forward transcribe request to Bot Manager.

get.description: Returns a list of all meetings initiated by the user associated with the API key.

get.summary: Get a single meeting by database ID
get.description: Forward to meeting-api GET /bots/id/{meeting_id}.

get.summary: Get transcript for a specific meeting
get.description: Retrieves the transcript segments for a meeting specified by its platform and native ID.

post.summary: Create a short-lived public URL for a transcript (for ChatGPT 'Read from URL')
post.description: Mints a random, short-lived share URL that anyone can read (no auth). Intended for passing transcript content to ChatGPT via a link.

get.description: Publicly accessible transcript content for a short-lived share ID. Fetches fresh transcript on each request. No auth. Intended for ChatGPT 'Read from URL'.

patch.description: Updates meeting metadata. Only name, participants, languages, and notes can be updated.

data.description: Schema for updating meeting data fields - restricted to user-editable fields only

languages.description: List of language codes detected/used in the meeting
languages.title: Languages

data.title: MeetingDataUpdate

requestBody.description: Meeting data to update (name, participants, languages, notes only)

delete.summary: Delete meeting transcripts and anonymize data
delete.description: Purges transcripts and anonymizes meeting data for finalized meetings. Only works for completed or failed meetings. Preserves meeting records for telemetry.

put.description: Sets a webhook URL for the authenticated user to receive notifications.

post.description: Forwards chat to agent-api. If session is meeting_aware, injects active meeting context.

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: A chat message from the meeting.

MeetingCompletionReason.title: MeetingCompletionReason
MeetingCompletionReason.description:
Reasons for meeting completion.

v0.10.5 Pack J — taxonomy expanded with two prod-derived values from
[PLATFORM] data on #255 (47% of `completed` meetings in 30d are actually
misclassified). The classifier already produces the correct reason; the
bug is downstream — meeting-api's exit-callback handler routed every
completion_reason to status='completed' regardless. Pack J's J.4 routing
rule (in callbacks.py) closes the silent-class.

id.title: Id
id.description: Internal database ID for the meeting

native_meeting_id.title: Native Meeting Id
native_meeting_id.description: The native meeting identifier provided during creation

constructed_meeting_url.title: Constructed Meeting Url
constructed_meeting_url.description: The meeting URL constructed internally, if possible

completion_reason.description: Reason the meeting concluded. Hoisted from data.completion_reason for typed-field access. None during in-flight states.

failure_stage.description: Lifecycle stage at which the meeting failed. Hoisted from data.failure_stage for typed-field access. None when status != failed.

data.title: Data
data.description: JSON data containing meeting metadata like name, participants, languages, notes, and status reasons

MeetingStatus.title: MeetingStatus
MeetingStatus.description:
Meeting status values with their sources and transitions.

Status Flow:
requested -> joining -> awaiting_admission -> active -> stopping -> completed
                                |              |                                                     v              v                  -> failed
                             failed         failed

Sources:
- requested: POST bot API (user)
- joining: bot callback
- awaiting_admission: bot callback  
- active: bot callback
- stopping: user (stop bot API)
- completed: user, bot callback
- failed: bot callback, validation errors

Platform.title: Platform
Platform.description:
Platform identifiers for meeting platforms.
The value is the external API name, while the bot_name is what's used internally by the bot.

id.title: Id
id.description: Internal database ID for the meeting

recordings.title: Recordings
recordings.description: Recording metadata attached to the meeting (if available).

notes.title: Notes
notes.description: Meeting notes (from meeting data, if provided).

TranscriptionResponse.title: TranscriptionResponse
TranscriptionResponse.description: Response for getting a meeting's transcript.

absolute_start_time.title: Absolute Start Time
absolute_start_time.description: Absolute start timestamp of the segment (UTC)

absolute_end_time.title: Absolute End Time
absolute_end_time.description: Absolute end timestamp of the segment (UTC)
